hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_97_309_705_788.caption: The Last Supper (Leonardo da Vinci)
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_97_309_705_788.answer: The Last Supper is a late 15th-century mural painting by Leonardo da Vinci. It is one of the most famous artworks of the Renaissance and shows the scene of the Last Supper of Jesus with his disciples.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_97_309_705_788.mcqQuestion: Study the artwork shown. Which statement correctly identifies The Last Supper and its importance?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_97_309_705_788.saQuestion: Identify the painting shown. Who painted it and why is it important in the Renaissance chapter?
options: The Last Supper is a famous Renaissance mural by Leonardo da Vinci depicting Jesus with his disciples.
The Industrial Revolution began in England in the 18th century and spread factory production worldwide.
Martin Luther posted the Ninety-five Theses in 1517 to protest against corruption in the Catholic Church.
The Storming of the Bastille in 1789 marked a major event of the French Revolution in Paris.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_883_489_1087_7.caption: Johannes Gutenberg
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_883_489_1087_7.answer: Johannes Gutenberg invented the printing press, which accelerated the Renaissance movement. Printing helped spread new ideas across Europe and made people question old beliefs.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_883_489_1087_7.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Johannes Gutenberg. Which statement about him is correct?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_883_489_1087_7.saQuestion: Identify Johannes Gutenberg shown in the figure. Explain how the printing press helped the Renaissance.
options: Johannes Gutenberg invented the printing press, which spread Renaissance ideas across Europe.
John Kay invented the flying shuttle in 1733 to speed up the weaving process.
Johannes Gutenberg led the Protestant Reformation by posting the Ninety-five Theses.
Johannes Gutenberg discovered the sea route from Portugal to India in 1498.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_642_1106_897_1.caption: Leonardo da Vinci
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_642_1106_897_1.answer: Leonardo da Vinci was a great Renaissance artist of Florence. He is known for paintings such as The Last Supper, and also for sketches, sculptures and scientific studies that reflect the spirit of inquiry of the Renaissance.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_642_1106_897_1.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Leonardo da Vinci. Which statement correctly describes him?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_642_1106_897_1.saQuestion: Identify Leonardo da Vinci shown in the portrait. Why is he regarded as an important Renaissance figure?
options: Leonardo da Vinci was a Renaissance artist of Florence known for painting, science and realistic art.
Michelangelo wrote the Ninety-five Theses and began the Protestant Reformation in Germany.
Leonardo da Vinci popularised the term Industrial Revolution for Britain from 1760 to 1840.
Leonardo da Vinci was the King of England who broke with the Roman Catholic Church.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_910_1112_1153_.caption: Michelangelo
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_910_1112_1153_.answer: Michelangelo was a famous Renaissance sculptor and painter. His statue of Pieta in Rome is a well-known example of Renaissance sculpture that beautifully captured the grace of the human body.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_910_1112_1153_.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Michelangelo. Which statement correctly describes his contribution?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_910_1112_1153_.saQuestion: Identify Michelangelo shown in the figure. Mention one major work associated with him in the Renaissance.
options: Michelangelo was a Renaissance sculptor; his statue of Pieta in Rome is a famous example of Renaissance art.
Michelangelo invented the power loom in 1785 during the Industrial Revolution.
Michelangelo was a German monk who translated the Bible to spread Lutheranism.
Michelangelo was an explorer who circumnavigated the world for Spain.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_942_306_1154_6.caption: Leonardo da Vinci — anatomical sketches
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_942_306_1154_6.answer: These anatomical and physiological sketches were made by Leonardo da Vinci. They show his scientific spirit and spirit of inquiry during the Renaissance, when scholars observed nature carefully to understand the human body.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_942_306_1154_6.mcqQuestion: Study Leonardo da Vinci's anatomical sketches. Which statement best explains their significance?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_942_306_1154_6.saQuestion: What do Leonardo da Vinci's anatomical sketches show about Renaissance thinking?
options: They show Leonardo's scientific observation of the human body, reflecting the Renaissance spirit of inquiry.
They were maps used by European explorers to discover new trade routes to Asia.
They were official documents used as primary sources of medieval church history.
They were propaganda posters supporting the rise of imperialism in Asia.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_901_1222_1164_.caption: Martin Luther
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_901_1222_1164_.answer: Martin Luther (1483–1546) of Germany was one of the greatest leaders of the Reformation. In 1517 he posted his Ninety-five Theses on the church door at Wittenberg, challenging corrupt church practices and sparking the Protestant Reformation.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_901_1222_1164_.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Martin Luther. Which statement correctly describes his role in the Reformation?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_901_1222_1164_.saQuestion: Identify Martin Luther shown in the figure. Why is he called a leader of the Protestant Reformation?
options: Martin Luther posted the Ninety-five Theses in 1517 and led the Protestant Reformation in Germany.
Martin Luther invented the printing press that spread Renaissance literature in Italy.
Martin Luther was the French king who signed the Atlantic Charter in 1941.
Martin Luther discovered America while searching for a western route to the East.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_158_198_358_47.caption: John Calvin
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_158_198_358_47.answer: John Calvin was an important Protestant reformer from France. The Reformation spread to France under Calvin, who challenged the authority of the Pope and supported reform of the Catholic Church.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_158_198_358_47.mcqQuestion: This portrait is of a leader of the Reformation. Which statement correctly identifies him?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_158_198_358_47.saQuestion: Identify the reformer shown. What role did John Calvin play in the spread of the Reformation?
options: John Calvin was a Protestant reformer through whom the Reformation spread in France.
John Calvin invented the steam engine that powered British factories.
John Calvin was the Aztec emperor who ruled Tenochtitlan in Mexico.
John Calvin was the first Prime Minister of independent India in 1947.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_509_196_709_48.caption: King Henry VIII
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_509_196_709_48.answer: King Henry VIII of England was an important figure in the Reformation. Along with Luther and Calvin, he challenged the authority of the Pope and helped the Reformation spread in England.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_509_196_709_48.mcqQuestion: This portrait is of a ruler linked with the English Reformation. Who is he?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_509_196_709_48.saQuestion: Identify King Henry VIII shown in the figure. How was he associated with the Reformation in England?
options: King Henry VIII of England challenged papal authority and supported the Reformation in England.
King Henry VIII led the Dandi March to protest against British salt laws in 1930.
King Henry VIII was an Inca ruler who built the city of Machu Picchu.
King Henry VIII was the chairman of the UN Security Council after World War II.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_331_724_593_10.caption: Arnold Toynbee
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_331_724_593_10.answer: Arnold Toynbee, a British economic historian, first popularised the term 'Industrial Revolution'. He used it to describe Britain's economic development from 1760 to 1840, marked by factories, machines and steam power.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_331_724_593_10.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Arnold Toynbee. Why is he mentioned in the Industrial Revolution section?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_331_724_593_10.saQuestion: Who was Arnold Toynbee and why is he associated with the Industrial Revolution?
options: Arnold Toynbee popularised the term Industrial Revolution for Britain's development from 1760 to 1840.
Arnold Toynbee invented the flying shuttle in 1733 to speed up weaving.
Arnold Toynbee was the leader of the Salt March from Sabarmati to Dandi.
Arnold Toynbee was the first President of the United States after 1776.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_115_53_369_401.caption: John Kay
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_115_53_369_401.answer: John Kay invented the flying shuttle in 1733. It speeded up the weaving process and was one of the major inventions that helped industrialise Britain.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_115_53_369_401.mcqQuestion: The figure shows John Kay. Which invention is associated with him?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_115_53_369_401.saQuestion: Identify John Kay and state the invention credited to him in the Industrial Revolution.
options: John Kay invented the flying shuttle in 1733, which speeded up weaving.
John Kay invented the power loom in 1785 for mechanised textile production.
John Kay posted the Ninety-five Theses to begin the Reformation.
John Kay discovered the sea route to India around the Cape of Good Hope.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_384_59_628_404.caption: Edmund Cartwright
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_384_59_628_404.answer: Edmund Cartwright invented the power loom in 1785. It was a mechanised loom that increased weaving speed and contributed to factory-based textile production during the Industrial Revolution.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_384_59_628_404.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Edmund Cartwright. Which statement about him is correct?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_384_59_628_404.saQuestion: Who was Edmund Cartwright and what did he invent during the Industrial Revolution?
options: Edmund Cartwright invented the power loom in 1785, increasing weaving speed in factories.
Edmund Cartwright invented the flying shuttle in 1733 for handloom weavers.
Edmund Cartwright was a Renaissance painter who created The Last Supper.
Edmund Cartwright was the Aztec ruler defeated when Tenochtitlan fell in 1521.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_661_48_900_400.caption: Karl Marx
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_661_48_900_400.answer: Karl Marx was a philosopher who, along with Friedrich Engels, supported the workers' demand for better conditions during the Industrial Revolution. Their ideas contributed to the rise of socialism.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_661_48_900_400.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Karl Marx. Which statement correctly links him to the Industrial Revolution?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_661_48_900_400.saQuestion: Identify Karl Marx shown in the figure. How is he connected with the social impact of industrialisation?
options: Karl Marx supported workers' demands for better conditions and helped inspire socialist ideas.
Karl Marx invented the cotton gin to separate cotton from its seeds in 1793.
Karl Marx was the British monarch who ruled during the Storming of the Bastille.
Karl Marx was appointed the first Prime Minister of India in 1947.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_922_45_1148_40.caption: Friedrich Engels
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_922_45_1148_40.answer: Friedrich Engels, along with Karl Marx, supported the growing interest in social revolution among industrial workers. Poor working conditions and exploitation during industrialisation led many thinkers to demand socialism.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_922_45_1148_40.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Friedrich Engels. Which statement correctly describes his relevance to this chapter?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_922_45_1148_40.saQuestion: Identify Friedrich Engels and explain his connection with socialism and the Industrial Revolution.
options: Friedrich Engels, with Karl Marx, supported socialist ideas arising from poor industrial working conditions.
Friedrich Engels invented the spinning jenny in 1764 for textile factories.
Friedrich Engels led the Protestant Reformation by translating the Bible into German.
Friedrich Engels was the chief justice who guards the Indian Constitution.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_73_1168_1109_1.caption: Machu Picchu (Inca Empire)
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_73_1168_1109_1.answer: Machu Picchu is a famous example of Inca architecture in South America. During the Renaissance period in Europe, the Inca Empire flourished in present-day Ecuador, Chile, Peru, Argentina and Bolivia and was renowned for exceptional architecture and terraced settlements.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_73_1168_1109_1.mcqQuestion: Study the picture of Machu Picchu. Which statement correctly describes the Inca Empire?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_73_1168_1109_1.saQuestion: Identify the monument shown. Which empire built it and where did that empire flourish?
options: Machu Picchu reflects Inca architecture; the Inca Empire flourished in western South America.
Machu Picchu was the capital of the Aztec Empire in present-day Mexico until 1521.
Machu Picchu was a factory built during the British Industrial Revolution.
Machu Picchu was the parliament building where the Atlantic Charter was signed.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_101_328_800_78.caption: Battle of Bunker Hill (American Revolution)
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_101_328_800_78.answer: This painting by John Trumbull shows the death of General Joseph Warren at the Battle of Bunker Hill (1775), also called the Battle of Breed's Hill. It was the first major battle of the American Revolution.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_101_328_800_78.mcqQuestion: Study the painting from the Age of Revolutions. Which event does it depict?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_101_328_800_78.saQuestion: Identify the battle shown in the painting. Why is it important in the American Revolution?
options: The Battle of Bunker Hill (1775), the first major battle of the American Revolution.
The Storming of the Bastille (1789), which began the French Revolution in Paris.
The Jallianwala Bagh Massacre of 1919 during the Indian national movement.
The signing of the Atlantic Charter on board a battleship in 1941.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_175_205_576_44.caption: Storming of the Bastille
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_175_205_576_44.answer: The Storming of the Bastille was a major event of the French Revolution. The French Revolution resulted from social, religious and economic injustices and led to the end of the monarchy in France.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_175_205_576_44.mcqQuestion: Study the figure labelled Storming of the Bastille. Which revolution is it associated with?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_175_205_576_44.saQuestion: Identify the event shown. Explain its significance in the French Revolution.
options: The Storming of the Bastille was a key event of the French Revolution against royal injustice.
Pulling down the statue of King George III marked a protest in the American Revolution.
The Battle of Bunker Hill was the first major battle of the American Revolution in 1775.
The Dandi March protested against the British monopoly over salt in 1930.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_703_206_1105_4.caption: Pulling down the statue of King George III
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_703_206_1105_4.answer: Pulling down the statue of King George III symbolises colonial resistance during the American Revolution. The American Revolution was one of the earliest modern revolutions in which American colonies rejected British rule and chose democracy.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_703_206_1105_4.mcqQuestion: Study the figure showing the pulling down of King George III's statue. Which revolution does it relate to?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_703_206_1105_4.saQuestion: What does pulling down the statue of King George III represent in the American Revolution?
options: It symbolises American rejection of British colonial rule during the American Revolution.
It shows French citizens storming the Bastille to end the monarchy in 1789.
It depicts Indians boycotting British goods in the Non-cooperation Movement.
It marks the signing of the Peace of Augsburg in Germany in 1555.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_58_1005_1093_1.caption: Impact of Imperialism on South Asia
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_58_1005_1093_1.answer: The British and French emerged as major rivals for trade in India from the 1500s. The British defeated the French in 1760 and later expanded their territory in India through wars, diplomacy and commercial activities.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_58_1005_1093_1.mcqQuestion: Study the figure on imperialism in South Asia. Which statement is correct?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_58_1005_1093_1.saQuestion: How did the British expand their power in India according to the textbook?
options: After defeating the French in 1760, the British expanded in India through wars, diplomacy and trade.
The Inca Empire was the largest empire in the world in the early 16th century.
Martin Luther posted the Ninety-five Theses in Wittenberg in 1517.
Johannes Gutenberg invented the printing press during the Renaissance in Italy.

hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_858_207_1057_5.caption: Friedrich Engels
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_858_207_1057_5.answer: Friedrich Engels, along with Karl Marx, supported the growing interest in social revolution among industrial workers. Poor working conditions and exploitation during industrialisation led many thinkers to demand socialism.
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_858_207_1057_5.mcqQuestion: The figure shows Friedrich Engels. Which statement correctly describes his relevance to this chapter?
hist-ch1/img_in_image_box_858_207_1057_5.saQuestion: Identify Friedrich Engels and explain his connection with socialism and the Industrial Revolution.
options: Friedrich Engels, with Karl Marx, supported socialist ideas arising from poor industrial working conditions.
Friedrich Engels invented the spinning jenny in 1764 for textile factories.
Friedrich Engels led the Protestant Reformation by translating the Bible into German.
Friedrich Engels was the chief justice who guards the Indian Constitution.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_228_1239_377_14.caption: Chandra Shekhar
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_228_1239_377_14.answer: Chandra Shekhar was a former Prime Minister of India. The Prime Minister is the head of the government, leader of the Council of Ministers, and is appointed by the President from the party or coalition commanding a majority in the Lok Sabha.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_228_1239_377_14.mcqQuestion: Who is the former Prime Minister of India shown in this portrait?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_228_1239_377_14.saQuestion: Identify Chandra Shekhar shown in the figure. Briefly explain the role of the Prime Minister in India's parliamentary democracy.
options: Chandra Shekhar was a former Prime Minister of India and head of the Council of Ministers.
Chandra Shekhar was the Chief Justice who interprets the Constitution as guardian of fundamental rights.
Chandra Shekhar was the President, the nominal constitutional head of the Indian State.
Chandra Shekhar was the Speaker who regulates proceedings only in the Rajya Sabha.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_386_1234_542_14.caption: P. V. Narasimha Rao
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_386_1234_542_14.answer: P. V. Narasimha Rao was a former Prime Minister of India. The Prime Minister is the real head of government, coordinates administration, formulates policies with the Cabinet, and represents India at international forums.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_386_1234_542_14.mcqQuestion: Who is the former Prime Minister of India shown in this portrait?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_386_1234_542_14.saQuestion: Identify P. V. Narasimha Rao shown in the figure. State two functions of the Prime Minister mentioned in your textbook.
options: P. V. Narasimha Rao was a former Prime Minister who headed the government and Council of Ministers.
P. V. Narasimha Rao was the Governor who headed the state executive in a Union territory.
P. V. Narasimha Rao was the Vice President and ex-officio Chairperson of the Rajya Sabha.
P. V. Narasimha Rao was a judge of the Supreme Court who declared laws unconstitutional.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_5_1241_218_1444.caption: V. P. Singh
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_5_1241_218_1444.answer: V. P. Singh was a former Prime Minister of India. The Prime Minister is appointed by the President and is usually the leader of the majority party or coalition in the Lok Sabha.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_5_1241_218_1444.mcqQuestion: Who is the former Prime Minister of India shown in this portrait?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_5_1241_218_1444.saQuestion: Identify V. P. Singh shown in the figure. How is the Prime Minister appointed in India?
options: V. P. Singh was a former Prime Minister appointed by the President from the Lok Sabha majority.
V. P. Singh was elected directly by all voters in a national presidential election.
V. P. Singh was the permanent head of the UN Security Council after World War II.
V. P. Singh was the Chief Election Commissioner who conducts Lok Sabha polls.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_548_1230_708_14.caption: I. K. Gujral
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_548_1230_708_14.answer: I. K. Gujral was a former Prime Minister of India. The Prime Minister distributes portfolios among ministers, coordinates departments, and acts as the vital link between the President and the Cabinet.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_548_1230_708_14.mcqQuestion: Who is the former Prime Minister of India shown in this portrait?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_548_1230_708_14.saQuestion: Identify I. K. Gujral shown in the figure. Mention any two responsibilities of the Prime Minister.
options: I. K. Gujral was a former Prime Minister who coordinated the work of ministers and departments.
I. K. Gujral was the Attorney General who defended the government only in the Supreme Court.
I. K. Gujral was the Comptroller and Auditor General who audits government accounts.
I. K. Gujral was the Director General of WHO responsible for global medical care.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_711_1226_871_14.caption: Atal Bihari Vajpayee
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_711_1226_871_14.answer: Atal Bihari Vajpayee was a former Prime Minister of India. The Prime Minister is the principal spokesperson of the government in Parliament and represents India at international conferences.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_711_1226_871_14.mcqQuestion: Who is the former Prime Minister of India shown in this portrait?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_711_1226_871_14.saQuestion: Identify Atal Bihari Vajpayee shown in the figure. What role does the Prime Minister play in Parliament and abroad?
options: Atal Bihari Vajpayee was a former Prime Minister and principal spokesperson of the government.
Atal Bihari Vajpayee was the Chief Minister who heads only a state council of ministers.
Atal Bihari Vajpayee was the Deputy Speaker who replaces the Lok Sabha Speaker permanently.
Atal Bihari Vajpayee was the chairman of the Trusteeship Council of the United Nations.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_872_1224_1050_1.caption: H. D. Deve Gowda
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_872_1224_1050_1.answer: H. D. Deve Gowda was a former Prime Minister of India. The Prime Minister forms policies with the Cabinet and runs the day-to-day administration of the country.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_872_1224_1050_1.mcqQuestion: Who is the former Prime Minister of India shown in this portrait?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_872_1224_1050_1.saQuestion: Identify H. D. Deve Gowda shown in the figure. Why is the Prime Minister called the head of government?
options: H. D. Deve Gowda was a former Prime Minister and head of government in India's parliamentary system.
H. D. Deve Gowda was the President who is only the nominal constitutional head of state.
H. D. Deve Gowda was a High Court judge who interprets laws at the state level only.
H. D. Deve Gowda was the Secretary General of the United Nations in New York.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_107_961_618_126.caption: Bombay High Court
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_107_961_618_126.answer: The Bombay High Court is part of the Indian judiciary. The judiciary interprets laws, protects the Constitution, and checks the working of the legislature and executive in a parliamentary democracy.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_107_961_618_126.mcqQuestion: Study the picture of the Bombay High Court. Which organ of government does it represent?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_107_961_618_126.saQuestion: Identify the institution shown. What is the main function of the judiciary in India?
options: The judiciary interprets laws and acts as a check on the legislature and executive.
The legislature makes laws through the Parliament and state legislatures only.
The executive implements laws through the Prime Minister and Council of Ministers.
The UN Security Council maintains international peace and security among member nations.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_692_208_1105_45.caption: Organs of the Indian Government
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_692_208_1105_45.answer: India has three organs of government: the Legislature (makes laws), the Executive (executes laws), and the Judiciary (interprets laws and checks the other two organs). Power is distributed between the Centre and the states in this parliamentary democracy.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_692_208_1105_45.mcqQuestion: Study the figure on organs of government. Which statement is correct?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_692_208_1105_45.saQuestion: Name the three organs of the Indian government and state one function of each.
options: Legislature makes laws, Executive executes laws, and Judiciary interprets laws and checks others.
Only the President makes, executes and interprets all laws without any separation of powers.
The UN General Assembly, Security Council and Secretariat are India's three organs of government.
Governors alone make laws at the Centre while MPs execute them in the states.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_772_28_1152_302.caption: Organs of the Indian Government
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_772_28_1152_302.answer: In India's parliamentary democracy, supreme power ultimately resides with the people. The Constitution distributes authority among the Legislature, Executive and Judiciary at the Centre and in the states.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_772_28_1152_302.mcqQuestion: Study the figure introducing organs of government. Which statement best describes India's system?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_772_28_1152_302.saQuestion: Explain what is meant by the three organs of government in India's democracy.
options: India is a parliamentary democracy with Legislature, Executive and Judiciary sharing power under the Constitution.
India follows a presidential system where the President directly makes all laws without Parliament.
India has only one organ of government because all power rests with the Prime Minister alone.
India's organs of government are the WHO, UNESCO and International Court of Justice.

civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_222_1284_581_13.caption: Functions of the Prime Minister and the Cabinet
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_222_1284_581_13.answer: The Prime Minister with the Cabinet formulates government policies, coordinates administration, advises the President on major appointments, distributes portfolios among ministers, and represents India in Parliament and at international conferences.
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_222_1284_581_13.mcqQuestion: Study the figure on functions of the Prime Minister and Cabinet. Which statement is correct?
civ-ch1/img_in_image_box_222_1284_581_13.saQuestion: List four functions of the Prime Minister and the Cabinet as shown in your textbook.
options: The PM and Cabinet formulate policies, coordinate administration, advise appointments and represent India.
The PM and Cabinet interpret the Constitution and declare laws null and void in all cases.
The PM and Cabinet conduct elections and count votes for the President's direct election.
The PM and Cabinet serve as permanent judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts.
